proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the 32 features has a non-zero univariate association with objective_response.
feature_030 is degenerate (single value) and provides no information.
feature_008 = 1 patients have a higher objective_response rate than feature_008 = 0 patients.
feature_013 = 1 patients have a lower objective_response rate than feature_013 = 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: feature_030 has nunique=1 (all values 0). Excluded from further analysis.
feature_008=1 RR=0.361 vs feature_008=0 RR=0.159; risk diff +0.202, chi-square p<1e-300. Strongly POSITIVE.
feature_013=1 RR=0.154 vs feature_013=0 RR=0.346; risk diff -0.192, chi-square p<1e-300. Strongly NEGATIVE.

analyses.code: logistic regression of objective_response on each feature individually
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen across 31 informative features. Strong signals (p<1e-50): feature_013 (NEG), feature_008 (POS), feature_015 (NEG), feature_022 (NEG), feature_001 (NEG, ordinal), feature_021 (NEG), feature_020 (NEG). Moderate (p<1e-5): feature_027 (NEG), feature_024 (NEG), feature_002 (POS). Several features show essentially null univariate signal: feature_005, _006, _011, _016 (age-like), _017, _019, _023, _004, _010 (Gleason-like, mean~7.6), _007 (sodium-like), _032 (calcium-like).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 retains a strong positive association with objective_response after adjusting for all other features (no confounding).
feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 each retain independent NEGATIVE associations with objective_response after multivariable adjustment.
feature_001 (3-level ordinal, 0/1/2) shows a per-level NEGATIVE association after adjustment (consistent with a performance-status / stage-like marker).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression with all 31 features (continuous standardized). Strongest adjusted effects on log-odds(response): feature_008 +1.20 (z=+52.6), feature_013 -1.13 (z=-45.4), feature_015 -0.69 (z=-22.1), feature_021 -0.74 (z=-16.9), feature_001 -0.22 per level (z=-19.3), feature_022 -0.10 per SD (z=-5.5), feature_020 -0.14 per SD (z=-12.1), feature_027 -0.43 (z=-6.0), feature_024 -0.07 per SD (z=-5.4), feature_002 +0.06 per SD (z=+5.6). All p<1e-7. Other features (016 age-like, 010 Gleason-like, 007 Na-like, etc.) non-significant.
feature_013 OR=0.32, feature_015 OR=0.50, feature_021 OR=0.48, feature_027 OR=0.65 (each adjusted).

proposed_hypotheses.text: All 12 binary features are essentially independent of one another (phi correlations near 0), consistent with randomized-like assignment rather than clinical-indication confounding.
feature_008 is the dominant 'treatment' feature, since it has by far the largest effect on response (OR>3) and is binary in ~40% of patients.

analyses.code: phi = sqrt(chi2/n) for each pair of binaries
analyses.result_summary: Among 66 binary-binary pairs the largest |phi| is 0.010 (feature_006 x feature_023, p=0.024). All other pairs |phi|<=0.009. Conclusion: binary features are jointly orthogonal.
feature_008 z=+52.6 (largest in absolute value); 2nd largest is feature_013 z=-45.4 (a negative prognostic marker).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008's effect on objective_response is suppressed (interaction) by feature_013 = 1 (patients with feature_013=1 derive less benefit from feature_008).
feature_008's effect is also suppressed by feature_015 = 1, feature_021 = 1, and feature_027 = 1.
feature_008's effect attenuates as continuous feature_022 increases.
Other binary features (005, 006, 011, 017, 019, 023, 004) and continuous features have no meaningful treatment-effect modification of feature_008.

analyses.result_summary: feature_008 main effect when feature_013=0: beta=+2.04 (p<1e-300); feature_008 x feature_013 interaction beta=-1.98 (p<1e-300). Total feature_008 effect when feature_013=1 is essentially 0.

All three interactions are large and highly significant: feature_008 x feature_015 beta_int=-1.24 (p=1.4e-93); feature_008 x feature_021 beta_int=-1.14 (p=3.3e-42); feature_008 x feature_027 beta_int=-1.28 (p=1.8e-19). Each effectively zeros out feature_008 when the partner is positive.
feature_008 x feature_022 (per SD) interaction beta=-0.81 (p=1.2e-72). Apparent attenuation in full population.

analyses.code: screen all binary x feature interactions for noise binaries
analyses.result_summary: For feature_005, _006, _011, _017, _019, _023, _004 the smallest p_int across all 31 covariates is 0.010 - 0.047, none surviving Bonferroni correction over 26+ tests. These features have no detectable treatment-effect heterogeneity (they are noise / balanced controls).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0, feature_008=1 patients have substantially higher objective_response rates than feature_008=0 patients.
In the complement (any one of feature_013, _015, _021, _027 equals 1), feature_008 has no detectable effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: fav = (f013==0)&(f015==0)&(f021==0)&(f027==0); compare RR by f008 within fav
analyses.result_summary: Favorable subgroup n=15,681 (31.4% of cohort). feature_008=1 (n=6325): RR=0.798. feature_008=0 (n=9356): RR=0.172. Risk difference +0.626 (chi-square p<1e-300; OR ~16.9).
Unfavorable subgroup n=34,319. feature_008=1 (n=13,751): RR=0.161. feature_008=0 (n=20,568): RR=0.153. Risk difference +0.008 (n.s.).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four predicates (feature_013=0, feature_015=0, feature_021=0, feature_027=0) is INDIVIDUALLY necessary: dropping any single one collapses the feature_008 effect to ~0.
Among the 16 cells of (feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027), only the all-zero cell shows a non-trivial feature_008 treatment effect.

analyses.code: tabulate feature_008 RR by (013,015,021,027) flipping one predicate at a time
analyses.result_summary: Relaxing feature_013 (others=0, _013=1): n=19,163, RR diff = +0.007 (vs +0.626 in base). Relaxing feature_015 (others=0, _015=1): n=3,940, RR diff = +0.021. Relaxing feature_021 (others=0, _021=1): n=1,775, RR diff = +0.004. Relaxing feature_027 (others=0, _027=1): n=494, RR diff = -0.025. All four predicates are individually necessary.

Among 16 cells of (013,015,021,027), only (0,0,0,0) shows a feature_008 effect of magnitude > 0.05 risk difference (it is +0.626). The next-largest is +0.088 (0,0,0,1; n=132, n.s.); rest are within ~+/-0.05 and statistically null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, the feature_008 treatment effect is approximately constant across feature_022 quartiles (no further continuous modifier).
Within the favorable subgroup, the feature_008 treatment effect is approximately constant across feature_001 levels.
Within the favorable subgroup, no continuous covariate substantially modifies the feature_008 treatment effect at Bonferroni-corrected significance.

analyses.code: stratify favorable subgroup by feature_022 quartile and recompute RR diff
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 Q1: diff=+0.633; Q2 +0.628; Q3 +0.624; Q4 +0.618. Interaction beta within favorable: -0.011 (p=0.79). Apparent feature_022 x feature_008 in full data was confounded by overlap with the four binary predicates.

analyses.code: stratify favorable subgroup by feature_001 level and recompute RR diff
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0: RR diff = +0.646 (n=5,511). =1: +0.622 (n=7,824). =2: +0.590 (n=2,346). Slight monotone attenuation; interaction beta=-0.077 (p=0.069). Effect remains very large at every level - feature_001 is not a subgroup-defining predicate.

analyses.code: screen all 26 covariates for feature_008 interaction within favorable
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-values within favorable: feature_005 (0.004), feature_017 (0.033), feature_026 (0.058), feature_001 (0.069). None survive Bonferroni (threshold 0.05/26 ~ 0.002).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the unfavorable subgroup, no individual predicate of {013, 015, 021, 027} alone produces a treatment effect for feature_008 (i.e. each is individually sufficient to extinguish the effect).
No additional binary or continuous feature recovers a feature_008 treatment effect inside the unfavorable subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013=1 only: n=19,163, diff=+0.007. feature_015=1 only: n=3,940, diff=+0.021. feature_021=1 only: n=1,775, diff=+0.004. feature_027=1 only: n=494, diff=-0.025. Each single-positive subgroup confirms suppression.

analyses.code: screen all covariates for feature_008 interaction within unfavorable
analyses.result_summary: Smallest p_int within unfavorable: feature_005 (0.004, beta=+0.18), feature_025 (0.06), feature_011 (0.09). None survive multiple-testing correction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (60% binary, no main effect) is not a treatment with hidden subgroup heterogeneity: no covariate x feature_005 interaction is significant after correction.
Same for feature_006, _011, _017, _019, _023, _004: these features show no treatment-effect heterogeneity at corrected significance.

analyses.code: interactions of feature_005 with all 30 other features
analyses.result_summary: Smallest interaction p-value for feature_005: 0.047 (with feature_027). Bonferroni threshold ~0.0017. Conclusion: no detectable subgroup effect.

feature_006 best p=0.010, feature_011 best p=0.023, feature_017 best p=0.030, feature_019 best p=0.010, feature_023 best p=0.031, feature_004 best p=0.033. None survive Bonferroni for ~30 tests. These features behave as null controls.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (0/1/2) shows a graded NEGATIVE association with response: response rate decreases monotonically across the three levels.
feature_022 (continuous, very right-skewed; max ~3622) shows a NEGATIVE association with response across its full range; log-transformed feature_022 fits the data.
feature_010 (integer 6-10, mean 7.6, consistent with Gleason score) shows essentially NO association with response in this cohort.
feature_016 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65; consistent with age) shows essentially NO association with response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0: RR 0.282; =1: 0.228; =2: 0.182. Logistic slope -0.285 per level (p=9.5e-73).
Univariate logistic on feature_022 (raw scale): beta=-0.0047 (p=1.6e-94); standardized OR=0.65 per SD.
feature_010 univariate beta=+0.005 (p=0.61). Cell rates: 6:0.244, 7:0.237, 8:0.242, 9:0.242, 10:0.242. No association.
feature_016 univariate beta=+0.00024 (p=0.82). No association with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS - feature_008 confers a large positive treatment effect on objective_response only when ALL of the following hold: feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0. In this subgroup, feature_008=1 raises objective_response from ~17% to ~80% (risk difference ~+0.63). Outside the subgroup (any of feature_013, _015, _021, _027 = 1), feature_008 has no effect.

The feature_008 treatment effect is monotonically attenuated by underlying continuous prognostic factors (feature_022, feature_001) only insofar as those factors lower BASELINE response; the multiplicative treatment effect in the favorable subgroup is approximately uniform.

analyses.result_summary: In the favorable subgroup (n=15,681): RR(8=1)=0.798, RR(8=0)=0.172, diff=+0.626, p<1e-300; OR ~16.9. Outside (n=34,319): RR(8=1)=0.161, RR(8=0)=0.153, diff=+0.008, p~0.17. The complete subgroup definition is feature_008=1 AND feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0; predicted response rate ~80%.

analyses.code: Within fav, regress y ~ feature_008 + feature_022_std + feature_001 + interactions
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable, feature_008 beta=+3.08 (z=+46.4); feature_022 main beta=-0.084 (p=0.008); feature_001 main beta=-0.352 (p<1e-16). Interactions feature_022 x 8 p=0.93; feature_001 x 8 p=0.078. Confirms continuous prognostic markers act on baseline, not on the feature_008 multiplicative effect.
